name: CRISPR Colony Picking Calculator
description: Given an expected editing efficiency, calculates how many colonies/clones to screen to be confident of recovering a desired number of successfully edited clones. Uses the binomial distribution to compute the smallest n such that P(>=k edits in n trials) >= confidence. Includes published efficiency presets for common contexts (Cas9 RNP/plasmid in mammalian cells, Cas9/Cas12a in E. coli, HDR) and a 1.5x safety bump for real-world losses (failed PCR, contamination, sequencing dropouts).

inputs.name: editing_efficiency
inputs.description: Expected fraction of colonies that will be successfully edited, in (0, 1]. Required unless preset is provided.

inputs.name: desired_clones
inputs.description: How many edited clones you want to recover. Default 1.

inputs.name: confidence
inputs.description: Target probability of recovering at least desired_clones. Default 0.95.

inputs.name: preset
inputs.description: Optional published-efficiency preset to use instead of supplying editing_efficiency. One of: 'cas9_rnp_mammalian', 'cas9_plasmid_mammalian', 'cas9_ecoli', 'cas12a_ecoli', 'hdr_mammalian', 'hdr_ecoli'.

inputs.name: max_colonies
inputs.description: Safety cap on the binomial search. Default 10000.

outputs.description: A dict with editing_efficiency used, desired_clones, confidence, colonies_to_pick (the answer), expected_edits, probability_at_chosen_n, safety_margin_recommendation (1.5x bump), and a plain-English recommendation paragraph.
